steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Determine the right angle and set up the tangent ratio
steps.content:
Since angles $A$ and $B$ are both acute in triangle $ABC$, the right angle must be at vertex $C$. This makes $AC$ and $BC$ the two legs of the right triangle.

For angle $B$, the tangent ratio is:
$$\tan(B) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{AC}{BC}$$

steps.title: Solve for BC
steps.content:
Substitute the known values ($\tan(B) = \frac{1}{9}$ and $AC = 29.5$):
$$\frac{1}{9} = \frac{29.5}{BC}$$
Cross-multiply to solve for $BC$:
$$BC = 29.5 \times 9 = 265.5$$
That confirms B is correct.
